description_long: This object is a segmented jersey barrier composed of several interlocking concrete sections arranged in a curved formation. Each segment has a trapezoidal profile with a broad, flat base and a slightly sloped top, typical of barriers used for traffic control and pedestrian safety. The surface appears to be unfinished concrete, giving it a rough, matte texture with a light gray coloration. The segments are closely aligned but not perfectly straight, suggesting modularity for flexible deployment in urban environments. There are no visible reflective strips, warning signs, or graffiti on the surface, and the barrier shows only minor scuffs consistent with outdoor use. The overall structure is robust and designed to act as a physical obstacle to vehicles and pedestrians.
description: A curved, segmented concrete jersey barrier with a rough, light gray surface and modular interlocking sections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curved alignment of the barrier segments and their trapezoidal profiles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness and depth of the individual segments, as well as the slight angle of the top surfaces.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous curve and the uniform height of the barrier segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the modular connections and the angular ends of the segments.
